NOTICE TO PLAINTIFF

LAW & MOTION, DEPT. 302, AS TO THE OCT-22-2024 HEARING RE: DEFENDANT SWATI SALUNKHE'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE THE DEFAULT AND DEFAULT JUDGMENT, THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING. MATTER IS TRANSFERRED TO BE HEARD IN DEPARTMENT 501 ON NOVEMBER 5, 2024, AT 9:30 A.M. THAT DEPARTMENT HANDLES ALL CASES ARISING FROM THE LANDLORD-TENANT DISPUTES PER SF LOCAL RULE 8.10A1. BOTH PARTIES TO COMPLY WITH SFLR 2.7B AND DELIVER COURTESY COPIES OF THEIR RESPECTIVE FILINGS IN SUPPORT OF AND IN OPPOSITION TO THE MOTION TO DEPARTMENT 501 WITH A COVER LETTER REFLECTING THE NEW HEARING DATE AND DEPARTMENT NO LATER THAN OCTOBER 25, 2024. JUDGE: RICHARD B. ULMER JR.; CLERK: S. KANE; NOT REPORTED. (302/RBU)

calendar.matters:
ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Sep-25-2025 Off Calendar

DEFAULT HEARING (PROVE UP)
Off Calendar Sep-22-2025 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This matter comes on calendar being set for plaintiff's request for entry of default judgment.

Judge Braden Woods; Clerk: Damon Carter; Dept. 505
Proceedings recorded by "For the Record" commencing at 9:23 a.m. and concluding at 10:06 a.m.

Plaintiffs Erik Johnson and Angelina Prokurdina appearing represented by Rahman Popal.
Defendants Swati Salunkhe and Vikrahm Salunkhe not appearing.

Court addresses Counsel for Plaintiff re: it's request for attorney fees. Court reviews declaration of counsel re: attorney fees.

Counsel for Plaintiff presents summary of case and an offer of proof.

Court examines Plaintiffs Erik Johnson and Angelina Prokudina and they confirms statements made by counsel.

The matter is submitted.

The court will take this matter under submission and parties were advised a judgment should be rendered by 9/19/25.

Mr. Popal emailed a proposed judgment to dept. 505 which was forwarded to Judge Woods.

Off Calendar Nov-05-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances, tentative ruling adopted as follows:

DEFENDANT SWATI SALUNKHE NOTICRE OF MOTION AND MOTION TO SET ASIDE THE DEFAULT AND DEFAULT JUDGMENT, ETC is DENIED. Moving party fails to carry their burden and show mistake, surprise or excusable neglect leading to a default. Defendant was served by registered process server.

Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: Vanessa Wu; Not Reported. (D501)

Noticre Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside The Default And Default Judgment, Etc.
Off Calendar Oct-22-2024 Continued To Nov-05-24 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 501
MINI MINUTES:

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT SWATI SALUNKHE'S Motion To Set Aside The Default And Default Judgment.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Matter is transferred to be heard in department 501 on November 5, 2024, at 9:30 a.m. That department handles all cases arising from the landlord-tenant disputes per SF Local Rule 8.10A1. Both parties to comply with SFLR 2.7B and deliver courtesy copies of their respective filings in support of and in opposition to the motion to department 501 with a cover letter reflecting the new hearing date and department no later than October 25, 2024.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: S. Kane; Not Reported. (302/RBU)
